Trust-region interior point method.
References
----------
.. [1] Byrd, Richard H., Mary E. Hribar, and Jorge Nocedal.
       "An interior point algorithm for large-scale nonlinear
       programming." SIAM Journal on Optimization 9.4 (1999): 877-900.
.. [2] Byrd, Richard H., Guanghui Liu, and Jorge Nocedal.
       "On the local behavior of an interior point method for
       nonlinear programming." Numerical analysis 1997 (1997): 37-56.
.. [3] Nocedal, Jorge, and Stephen J. Wright. "Numerical optimization"
       Second Edition (2006).
equality_constrained_sqp)
LinearOperator
tr_interior_pointc
d d!
BarrierSubproblemz
    Barrier optimization problem:
        minimize fun(x) - barrier_parameter*sum(log(s))
        subject to: constr_eq(x)     = 0
                  constr_ineq(x) + s = 0
    c

Z/usr/lib64/python3.9/site-packages/scipy/optimize/_trustregion_constr/tr_interior_point.py
__init__
BarrierSubproblem.__init__c
update:
BarrierSubproblem.updatec
	get_slack>
BarrierSubproblem.get_slackc
get_variablesA
BarrierSubproblem.get_variablesc
Returns barrier function and constraints at given point.
        For z = [x, s], returns barrier function:
            function(z) = fun(x) - barrier_parameter*sum(log(s))
        and barrier constraints:
            constraints(z) = [   constr_eq(x)     ]
                             [ constr_ineq(x) + s ]
        )
c_eq
c_ineqr$
function_and_constraintsD
z*BarrierSubproblem.function_and_constraintsc
inf)
s_ir$
<listcomp>]
z7BarrierSubproblem._compute_function.<locals>.<listcomp>)
sum)
log_sr$
z#BarrierSubproblem._compute_functionc
hstack)
z!BarrierSubproblem._compute_constrc
zZReturns scaling vector.
        Given by:
            scaling = [ones(n_vars), s]
        c
diag_elementsr$
matveco
z)BarrierSubproblem.scaling.<locals>.matvec)
onesr
scalingf
BarrierSubproblem.scalingc
Returns scaled gradient.
        Return scaled gradient:
            gradient = [             grad(x)             ]
                       [ -barrier_parameter*ones(n_ineq) ]
        and scaled Jacobian matrix:
            jacobian = [  jac_eq(x)  0  ]
                       [ jac_ineq(x) S  ]
        Both of them scaled by the previously defined scaling factor.
        )
J_eq
J_ineqr$
gradient_and_jacobianu
z'BarrierSubproblem.gradient_and_jacobianc
z#BarrierSubproblem._compute_gradientc
spsZ
issparse
csr_matrix
_assemble_sparse_jacobianr3
diag
zerosr
toarray
block)
z#BarrierSubproblem._compute_jacobianc
Assemble sparse Jacobian given its components.
        Given ``J_eq``, ``J_ineq`` and ``s`` returns:
            jacobian = [ J_eq,     0     ]
                       [ J_ineq, diag(s) ]
        It is equivalent to:
            sps.bmat([[ J_eq,   None    ],
                      [ J_ineq, diag(s) ]], "csr")
        but significantly more efficient for this
        given structure.
        Z
csr)
dtyper
FTN)

z$BarrierSubproblem.lagrangian_hessianc	
Stop criteria to the barrier problem.
        The criteria here proposed is similar to formula (2.3)
        from [1]_, p.879.
        TN)

| |!|#|"|$|

}#}$q
}'|'|
Trust-region interior points method.
    Solve problem:
        minimize fun(x)
        subject to: constr_ineq(x) <= 0
                    constr_eq(x) = 0
    using trust-region interior point method described in [1]_.
    g
maximumr>
maxZ	tr_radiusr(
